summary: PR #2713 linked src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c by marking the object Matching, updating ftmaterial symbols/data labels, adding a local ft_MObjInfo overlay for ftMObj-adjacent template data, adjusting ftMaterial_800BF2B8's signature/header, and making stack/code-shape changes in ftMaterial_800BF6BC. Bot report evidence says ftMaterial_800BF2B8 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC became new 100% matches, but reviewers objected to the stack-relative GXColor pointer arithmetic used to match ftMaterial_800BF6BC.

key_files.role: Main matching source changes for ftMaterial functions; introduced ft_MObjInfo overlay, signature/body reshaping, template field access, padding locals, and contested stack pointer arithmetic.
Header prototype updated for ftMaterial_800BF2B8 to include a third unused u32 parameter.
Changed ftmaterial.c object status from NonMatching to Matching.
Updated ftmaterial-related data/sdata labels, scopes, and sizes around ftMObj, ftMaterial_803C69D0, ftMaterial_803C6A44, and local string labels.

what_changed: Marked Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftmaterial.c") as Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftmaterial.c") in configure.py.

Added local struct ft_MObjInfo containing HSD_MObjInfo parent, HSD_TevDesc tevdesc_tmpl, and HSD_TECnst texp_tmpl, then replaced raw base+offset template reads from &ftMObj with info->tevdesc_tmpl and info->texp_tmpl.
Changed ftMaterial_800BF2B8 from two parameters to three parameters, adding u32 unused in both source and header, and cast ftMObj.setup assignment through HSD_MObjSetupFunc/Event.
Reused the rendermode parameter as the mutable render mode value instead of a separate mobj_rendermode local, and introduced a new_texp temporary before assigning texp1.

In ftMaterial_800BF6BC, replaced PAD_STACK(160) and prior locals with explicit padding arrays and an unused GXColor, then used stack-relative pointer arithmetic `(GXColor*) ((u8*) &sp_tevdesc - 4)` for a color value.
Updated symbols.txt to split/rename ftmaterial data around .data:0x803C69D0 and .data:0x803C6A44 and to convert some ftCo string globals at .sdata:0x804D3C00/.sdata:0x804D3C08 to local @ labels.
PR bot report recorded +1804 matched code bytes, +2724 linked code bytes, +352 linked data bytes, and two new matches in main/melee/ft/ftmaterial: ftMaterial_800BF2B8 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC.

smart_moves.text: Using a small local overlay struct for ftMObj-adjacent template data made repeated `base + 0x50` and `base + 0xC4` reads more typed and reviewable.
Updating the public header when ftMaterial_800BF2B8's recovered signature changed kept the declaration aligned with the body.
The configure.py status flip was paired with bot report evidence showing two ftmaterial functions reached 100%.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a global object is followed by related template records in data, a narrow temporary struct overlay can express repeated offset loads better than raw char* base pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replacements of `(HSD_TECnst*) (base + 0xC4)` and `(HSD_TevDesc*) (base + 0x50)` with `info->texp_tmpl` and `info->tevdesc_tmpl`

decomp_lessons.lesson: A matching result can still contain review-risk codegen tactics; reviewer comments specifically flagged stack-relative local pointer arithmetic as needing a better match strategy.
Recovered function signatures should be reflected in the owning header rather than only changing the C definition.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftMaterial_800BF2B8 signature changed in source and header

naming_conventions.name: ftMaterial_803C69D0 / ftMaterial_803C6A44
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR used ftMaterial_ address-style names for ftmaterial-owned data rather than inventing semantic names for the template objects.

naming_conventions.name: struct ft_MObjInfo
naming_conventions.lesson: The local struct name mirrors the ft material/object context and exists to type the ftMObj-adjacent layout used by this TU.
Some prior lbl_/ftCo_ string symbols were converted to local @ labels, indicating the PR did not preserve global semantic names for ordinary local strings in this area.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Object status flip to Matching after focused function/data reshaping.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.evidence: configure.py changed ftmaterial.c from NonMatching to Matching; bot reported ftMaterial_800BF2B8 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC as new 100% matches.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Low as an intake fact; exact local verification beyond bot report is not independently reproduced here.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a local overlay struct to align codegen while avoiding repeated raw data offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.evidence: Added struct ft_MObjInfo and cast &ftMObj to access tevdesc_tmpl and texp_tmpl fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Medium; evidence supports the tactic in this PR, but broader layout ownership should be curated as a path/source fact before reuse.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add explicit padding arrays and an unused local GXColor in ftMaterial_800BF6BC to shape the stack.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: High; active standards require explicit evidence for padding/dummy locals, and reviewer comments objected to the nearby stack arithmetic.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use stack-relative pointer arithmetic to target a GXColor slot before sp_tevdesc.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.evidence: Introduced `(GXColor*) ((u8*) &sp_tevdesc - 4)` with a TODO comment; two reviewers questioned this hunk.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: High; conflicts with typed-access and matching-tactic caution unless no cleaner source shape exists and objdiff evidence is recorded.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.interpreted_topic: Reviewer skepticism toward the new stack-relative GXColor pointer arithmetic in ftMaterial_800BF6BC.

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.comment: there HAS to be a better way to match this than something like this.
review_feedback.interpreted_topic: Explicit request to find a cleaner matching strategy than pointer arithmetic from &sp_tevdesc - 4.

follow_up_queries: ftMaterial_800BF6BC stack pointer arithmetic reviewer better way

notes: The PR body is empty, so author rationale for tactics and naming is not available.
The bot report provides PR-local matching/linking evidence, but this intake did not independently run objdiff or builds.
Two review comments clearly object to the stack pointer arithmetic hunk, but the slice does not include later discussion resolving whether it was accepted as-is or changed after the excerpt.
Merged state is present in metadata, but merge does not by itself make every tactic an accepted decomp standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use narrow local overlay structs for adjacent template data instead of repeated raw offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2713, ftmaterial replaced `char* base = (char*) &ftMObj` plus offset casts with `struct ft_MObjInfo* info = (struct ft_MObjInfo*) &ftMObj` and field accesses `info->tevdesc_tmpl` / `info->texp_tmpl`. This is a candidate example of typed access improving reviewability while matching.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Stack-relative local pointer arithmetic is review-risk even if it matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2713, reviewers questioned the `GXColor* color = (GXColor*) ((u8*) &sp_tevdesc - 4)` tactic in ftMaterial_800BF6BC, with one reviewer saying there had to be a better way to match it.

source_update_candidates.title: Add explicit example discouraging stack-relative local pointer arithmetic
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an example under typed-fields-over-pointer-math or matching-tactics-need-evidence: avoid retaining constructs like `(GXColor*) ((u8*) &some_stack_local - 4)` to address another stack slot unless there is explicit objdiff evidence and no cleaner local declaration/lifetime shape. PR #2713 reviewers objected to this exact tactic.
source_update_candidates.reason: The standards already warn about pointer math and dummy locals, but this PR supplies a concrete reviewed example that may be useful for future QA hints.

source_update_candidates.title: ftmaterial ftMObj-adjacent template layout
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2713 modeled `&ftMObj` as `struct ft_MObjInfo { HSD_MObjInfo parent; HSD_TevDesc tevdesc_tmpl; HSD_TECnst texp_tmpl; }` and used this to access `tevdesc_tmpl` and `texp_tmpl` in ftMaterial_800BF534 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped context for future work touching ftmaterial data layout, but should be verified against current source/symbol ownership before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the stack pointer arithmetic hunk as an accepted tactic; reviewers explicitly questioned it and the slice does not show a clean resolution.
Do not infer broad symbol naming policy from this PR alone; symbols.txt changes are localized to ftmaterial data/string labels.
Do not treat the merge as proof that padding arrays or dummy locals are generally acceptable without additional verifier evidence.
